coaching.coaching: Plan how acceptance criteria completeness and coverage will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria completeness and coverage

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_apply_phases.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_apply_phases.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/dispatch_one.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `grep -c dispatch_one .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt docs/architecture.md` greps the unanchored word 'dispatch_one' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_dispatch_one'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"dispatch_one":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact test edit for `test_identity_264f_reporter.py`: add `import rebar_reconciler.dispatch_apply_phases as dispatch_phases` and repoint both `monkeypatch.setattr` calls to `dispatch_phases, '_record_reporter_alert', ...`; confirm that re-exporting `_record_reporter_alert` from `dispatch_one` is NOT sufficient (the patch must be on the module that owns the live binding).
evidence: AC6 re-exports: 'dispatch_one._update_one_apply_reporter, dispatch_one._update_one_filter_fields, dispatch_one._update_one_dispatch_comments still resolve' — _record_reporter_alert is absent from this list.
test_identity_264f_reporter.py line 67: monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, '_record_reporter_alert', ...) where dispatch = rebar_reconciler.dispatch_one

dispatch_one.py lines 604, 650, 662: _record_reporter_alert is defined and called only within _update_one_apply_reporter — it moves to dispatch_apply_phases but is not re-exported back to dispatch_one.

Plan says 'repoints...monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, "_record_reporter_alert", ...) to the leaf module' but does not specify a new import of dispatch_apply_phases in the test file, nor the exact new setattr target module object.
findings.finding: _record_reporter_alert is NOT included in the AC6 re-export list, but the plan says to repoint the test's monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, '_record_reporter_alert', ...) 'to the leaf module' without specifying what the new import or setattr target looks like. After the move, dispatch_one will not have _record_reporter_alert as an attribute (it is not in the re-export list), so the existing test patching dispatch._record_reporter_alert will raise AttributeError, and the test-edit description is underdetermined (no new import of dispatch_apply_phases is described).
findings.location: "Seam" section / AC6 re-export list / 'The one required test edit repoints...' paragraph
scenarios: After the move, `monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, '_record_reporter_alert', ...)` raises AttributeError because dispatch_one no longer owns that name.

Even if _record_reporter_alert is re-exported into dispatch_one, patching it there won't intercept calls inside dispatch_apply_phases._update_one_apply_reporter (which calls its own module-local _record_reporter_alert), so the test would silently pass without actually intercepting the alert write.
findings.suggested_fix: In test_identity_264f_reporter.py, add `import rebar_reconciler.dispatch_apply_phases as dispatch_phases` and change both `monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, '_record_reporter_alert', ...)` calls to `monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch_phases, '_record_reporter_alert', ...)`. Do NOT re-export _record_reporter_alert from dispatch_one (it is an internal helper).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC6, e.g. `python -c 'from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import dispatch_one; assert callable(dispatch_one._update_one_apply_reporter) and callable(dispatch_one._update_one_filter_fields) and callable(dispatch_one._update_one_dispatch_comments)'`
evidence: 'back-compat: dispatch_one._update_one_apply_reporter, dispatch_one._update_one_filter_fields, dispatch_one._update_one_dispatch_comments still resolve (re-exported)' — no verify: clause provided
findings.finding: AC6 (back-compat re-export) states that `dispatch_one._update_one_apply_reporter`, `dispatch_one._update_one_filter_fields`, and `dispatch_one._update_one_dispatch_comments` 'still resolve' but provides no proving command. An agent could ship a broken re-export and still pass all other ACs.
scenarios: Agent adds re-export lines but misspells one; AC5 (rebar_reconciler tests) passes because the functions are called via the leaf module, not via dispatch_one attribute access, so the broken re-export goes undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Append `(verify: python -c 'from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import dispatch_one; assert callable(dispatch_one._update_one_apply_reporter) and callable(dispatch_one._update_one_filter_fields) and callable(dispatch_one._update_one_dispatch_comments)')` to the AC6 bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC verifying the monkeypatch repoint, e.g. `grep -n 'dispatch_apply_phases.*_record_reporter_alert' tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_identity_264f_reporter.py` returns at least one match (verify the setattr now targets the leaf module).
evidence: 'The one required test edit repoints test_identity_264f_reporter.py's monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, "_record_reporter_alert", ...) to the leaf module' — described in body only, absent from AC list
ABSENCE: no AC reads 'test_identity_264f_reporter.py monkeypatch targets dispatch_apply_phases._record_reporter_alert'
findings.finding: The required edit to `test_identity_264f_reporter.py` (repointing `monkeypatch.setattr(dispatch, '_record_reporter_alert', ...)` to the leaf module) is described in the plan body but is NOT captured as an acceptance criterion, so there is no measurable, verifiable gate ensuring this change was made.
findings.location: Plan body — last paragraph before Acceptance Criteria; Acceptance Criteria section (omission)
scenarios: Agent skips the monkeypatch repoint; the test may still pass if _record_reporter_alert is also re-exported via dispatch_one (monkeypatch on the old path still patches the right live object), masking the fact that the canonical location of the symbol is now the leaf module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / sequencing note: e.g., 'create dispatch_apply_phases.py and re-exports first, run full test suite to confirm green, THEN remove allowlist/ceilings/arch.md entries in the same or a follow-on commit; rollback = revert the leaf creation and restore the allowlist entries.'
evidence: 'dispatch_one.py absent from .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt; its docs/architecture.md offenders remedy row deleted'
ABSENCE: no sequencing instruction (e.g. 'create leaf and re-exports first, validate tests, THEN remove allowlist entries') and no back-out plan stated anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan performs a destructive step (deleting the dispatch_one allowlist/ceilings entries and arch.md row) as part of the same change as the code move, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out sequencing. If the new leaf is incomplete or the re-exports are wrong, CI fails with no stated recovery path beyond an implicit 'revert the commit.'
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 3 / 'Deviation from the architecture.md remedy row' section
scenarios: Implementer creates leaf file but forgets or mis-specifies a re-export, removes the allowlist entry, and CI immediately fails the module-size contract with no allowlist safety net and no documented recovery step.
Partial commit is merged; rollback requires knowing which files to touch, but the plan gives no guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Sequencing / Rollback' subsection: (1) create dispatch_apply_phases.py with moved symbols; (2) add re-exports in dispatch_one.py; (3) run `pytest` and `make lint` to confirm green; (4) only then delete the allowlist/ceilings entries and arch.md row. Rollback: revert steps 1–2 and restore the allowlist entries.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
